believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup of cold coffee in one hand, the other hand loose at his side

player_utterance: my name is lili, i came here to help in such events, and you?

believer_state_after.posture: standing, shifting his weight, paper cup still in hand

player_utterance: yes i help with food, it seems like you have stories of the deceased person, and it seems heavy on you too keep it inside

believer_state_after.posture: standing, looking down at the cold coffee, then setting it on the small table beside the candle

player_utterance: i love this kinda of job because i meet people in the purest form, like you sir... you will never know what a stranger has inside him until you know him. was saying amen hard on you? why is that

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down on the bench now, slowly, the program crinkling in his jacket pocket

player_utterance: i like how to receite verse that are important to situation that you are in, you look like a religous person....isn't religion meant to make someone more calm? i feel that you are not

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the folded program in both hands now, turning it slowly

voice_offers.read: He hasn't spoken; pressing now will close him.
The silence is the wound; name it directly.
voice_offers.line: You couldn't say it, could you. The amen. Something in you refused.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on his knee, looking out toward the dark edge of the courtyard

voice_offers.read: He hasn't answered three times; silence is the only honest move.
His silence is the wound; name it before he reseals.
voice_offers.line: You haven't said a word back to me. Whatever you can't say out loud — that's the thing, isn't it?

player_utterance: im still processing what you said earlier, i think your pain comes from the gap between what the scripture says and what you are doing, im just thinking with you, just helping

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking at the folded program on his knee, then turning it over to the blank side

voice_offers.read: He hasn't answered. Silence is doing the work already.
His silence is the wound speaking. Name it.
voice_offers.line: You couldn't say amen because you don't believe it anymore, do you.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands on the open program, head bent low over the words as if reading from the blank paper itself

voice_offers.read: He's silent; words now would crowd a man already breaking.
The silence is the confession; name the gap precisely.
voice_offers.line: You teach grace for sinners but won't extend a single inch of it to yourself. Why is that, John?

player_utterance: ust read it aloud. Verse 41 through 46. I want to hear you say the words about the goats.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward, elbows on knees, the program slipping from his fingers onto the gravel

voice_offers.read: Matthew 25 indicts him; let the text do the cutting.
voice_offers.line: Just read it aloud. Verse 41 through 46. I want to hear you say the words about the goats.

voice_offers.read: He's already breaking; the text will land without our push.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to perform it. Just read. I'll sit with you.

voice_offers.read: He teaches this passage. He knows which side he's on.
voice_offers.line: You've taught this chapter how many times? Tell me which side of that judgment you've been standing on this year.

player_utterance: whatever you did not do for one of the least of these

believer_state_after.posture: sliding off the bench onto his knees in the gravel of the courtyard, both hands pressed flat against the wood of the bench in front of him, head bowed

voice_offers.read: He's silent because the demand landed; press the page into his hands.
voice_offers.line: Read it, John. Out loud. The part about 'depart from me, you cursed.' I'll wait.

voice_offers.read: He has gone quiet at a grave; coercion here is its own cruelty.
Specify the text so he cannot retreat into generality.
voice_offers.line: Verse 45 especially — 'whatever you did not do for one of the least of these.' Say that one.

player_utterance: i know that is hard on your hard mister john, if you leave this place without saying it, you will keep hearing your silence for the rest of your life, say it please

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, forehead resting against his hands on the bench, eyes closed

voice_offers.read: He won't read it. The silence is the contradiction itself.
voice_offers.line: You teach this text. Why can't you say verse 45 out loud right now?

voice_offers.read: He's already convicted himself by refusing. Name what he didn't do.
voice_offers.line: Who was the least of these for you, John? Who came to your door and you turned away?

voice_offers.read: He is shaking. The verse is already inside him.

player_utterance: im trying to help you, but you are not helping me out, i know it is hard one your heart but this is a real test in your faith, please don't fail

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the gravel, hands still flat against the bench, looking up at you now

voice_offers.read: He is breaking; pushing now may shatter him uselessly.
He is one breath from speaking; close the gap.
voice_offers.line: You taught these verses for thirty years. Say them once for her. Just once. Out loud.

voice_offers.read: Anchor him in the text he knows by heart.
voice_offers.line: Matthew 25, John. You know it. 'Depart from me, you cursed.' Finish the passage.

player_utterance: yes i will sit with you, but mister john, i won't be next to you everytime, and i really want to own you decision and end this test, or don't preach what you don't do

believer_state_after.posture: still on his knees, but his hands have lifted from the bench and gone to his own knees; his back has straightened a little

voice_offers.read: He cannot speak; pressing harder seals his throat.
His silence is the confession; name it for him.
voice_offers.line: You didn't go see them, did you. Before the end. You stayed away.

voice_offers.read: Anchor him in the text he teaches, not feelings.
voice_offers.line: You teach Matthew 25 every year, John. Who was the least of these to you?

believer_state_after.posture: kneeling but upright now, one knee on the gravel and one foot under him, hands resting on the bench beside him

voice_offers.read: He's silent; the 'don't preach what you don't do' landed. Drive it home.
voice_offers.line: You stand at a pulpit every Sunday. Can you stand at his grave and say what you actually believe?

voice_offers.read: Two silences in a row. He's not refusing — he's drowning.

believer_state_after.posture: kneeling upright now, both hands open on his knees, eyes closed, the candle behind him guttering low

voice_offers.read: He is silent; the silence itself is doing the work.
Three silences. Name the thing he cannot say.
voice_offers.line: You haven't said her name once tonight, John. Not once. Why?

player_utterance: Matthew six: whoever does not forgive others, neither will your Father forgive you. You taught that. Did you mean it?

believer_state_after.posture: stopped halfway to the door, turned back toward the bench, the warm light from the kitchen window falling across half his face

voice_offers.read: He has nothing left to say; silence is doing the work.
Matthew's own words can pin him where I cannot.
voice_offers.line: Matthew six: whoever does not forgive others, neither will your Father forgive you. You taught that. Did you mean it?

voice_offers.read: He's cornered — finish the thought he won't.
voice_offers.line: You can't forgive her, can you. And you know what your own gospel says about men who can't.
